mcqs.question: What is the primary function of the Network Layer?

options.text: Error detection and correction
Routing and congestion control
Flow control and error control
Packet switching and routing

mcqs.explanation: The Network Layer is responsible for routing data between different networks and providing congestion control.

mcqs.question: What is the size of an IPv4 address?

options.text: 16 bits
32 bits
64 bits
128 bits

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Type of Service field in the IPv4 header?

options.text: To specify the protocol used in the datagram
To specify the type of service required
To specify the source IP address
To specify the destination IP address

mcqs.explanation: The Type of Service field specifies the type of service required for the datagram.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Fragment Offset field in the IPv4 header?

options.text: To specify the offset of the fragment in the original datagram
To specify the length of the fragment
To specify the protocol used in the datagram
To specify the source IP address

mcqs.explanation: The Fragment Offset field specifies the offset of the fragment in the original datagram.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Time to Live field in the IPv4 header?

options.text: To specify the time to live of the datagram
To specify the protocol used in the datagram
To specify the source IP address
To specify the destination IP address

mcqs.explanation: The Time to Live field specifies the time to live of the datagram.
